{{Quote|In recognition of your status as a Galladinium Preferred Customer, the following items are being offered to you exclusively through this special datalog edition. These items will not appear in our standard merchandise datalog available to the general public.|Introduction to the section of [[Galladinium's Galactic Datalog of Fantastic Technology|''Galladinium's Datalog'']] including the microbe armor|<ref name="GF" />}}
The '''Barabel microbe armor''' was a sleeveless armor developed by [[Creshaldyne Industries/Legends|Creshaldyne Industries]]. The suit had inflated pouches containing microbes that consumed [[Radiation/Legends|radiation]]. These beings fed on [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] bolts, and in fact bred when receiving an impact—increasing the protection of the vest. However, microbe armor was vulnerable to extreme cold.

The use of this armor was not widespread among [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] as it was useless against any other weapon. The item was offered to [[Galladinium Preferred Customers]] through [[Galladinium's Galactic Datalog of Fantastic Technology|''Galladinium's Datalog'']].

==Description==
The [[Barabel/Legends|Barabel]] microbe armor—sometimes known as microbe armor—developed by [[Creshaldyne Industries/Legends|Creshaldyne Industries]],<ref name="all">''[[Galladinium's Fantastic Technology]]'', ''[[Arms & Equipment Guide]]'', ''[[The New Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]'' and ''[[Galaxy at War]]''</ref> was a three-[[Kilogram/Legends|kilogram]]<ref name="AG" /> light suit of protective covering.<ref name="AGGW">''[[Arms & Equipment Guide]]''; [[Galaxy at War]]''</ref> It looked like a flexible<ref name="GW">''[[Galaxy at War]]''</ref> sleeveless vest<ref name="all" /> with several soft,<ref name="AG" /> external, sealed pockets<ref name="AGNG">''[[Arms & Equipment Guide]]'', ''[[The New Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]''</ref> or inflated bags.<ref name="GF" /> Each of those contained a protective ablative shell<ref name="GF" /> with a saline solution rich in<ref name="all" /> colonies of<ref name="NG" /> microorganisms, counted in the millions.<ref name="all" /> Those microbes fed on<ref name="GFG">''[[Galladinium's Fantastic Technology]]'', ''[[Arms & Equipment Guide]]''</ref> certain forms of energy emision<ref name="AGNG" /> and [[Radiation/Legends|radiation]], including [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] bolts<ref name="all" /> and extreme heat.<ref name="GW" /> When facing those particles, the microbes diffused it on contact<ref name="GFG" /> and at the same time they bred quickly. As the vest would then have a greater number of microbes, its resistance to radiation was also increased.<ref name="GFGNG">''[[Galladinium's Fantastic Technology]]'', ''[[Arms & Equipment Guide]]'' and ''[[The New Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]''</ref>

The microbes could feed on great amounts of radiation, but their lifespan covered only [[Standard second|seconds]] before they [[Death/Legends|died]].<ref name="GF" /> This, along with several environmental built-in controls, prevented them from overcrowding their bags.<ref name="AG" /> Again due to this, the increased protection after feeding on an impact was only temporary.<ref name="AGNG" /> Whenever the microbes were not breeding, they were lethargic,<ref name="GF" /> and were very susceptible to cold temperatures. All the microbes in a vest died after a fifteen-[[Standard minute/Legends|minute]] exposure to a temperature below 0 [[Celcius|degrees Celsius]],<ref name="GFG" /> and were useless as protection against weapons other than blasters.<ref name="NG" />

==History==
Creshaldyne Industries, the company that created the microbe armor, had successfully produced and marketed blast-dampening armors.<ref name="NG" /> It offered the microbe armor as a cloth whose protective efficiency increased as it received enemy impacts.<ref name="GF" />

Available, albeit not readily, during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the microbe armor was sometimes priced at 15,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]], and a recharge of new microbes was worth 12,000 credits more.<ref name="AG" /> The merchandise catalog ''[[Galladinium's Galactic Datalog of Fantastic Technology]]'' offered this item only to [[Galladinium Preferred Customers]], for 30,000 credits.<ref name="GF" /> The item was not recommended by adventurers and [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]], mostly because it was only useful against blasters.<ref name="NG" />

==Behind the scenes==
The microbe armor first appeared in ''[[Galladinium's Fantastic Technology]]'' ([[1995]]), a supplement to [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'', including an illustration of the item by [[Robert Duchlinski]]. It then appeared in ''[[Arms & Equipment Guide]]'' ([[2002]]), for [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'', and then in ''[[Galaxy at War]]'' ([[2009]]), for Wizards' [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Saga Edition Core Rulebook|''Saga Edition'']].

The item thus had descriptions and role-playing stats—albeit different ones—for all the official Star Wars role-playing games. The differences included ''Galaxy at War'' suggesting that the pockets were inside the vest; and ''Galladium'' not specifying at all that a destroyed vest could be recharged with new microbes. No source has specified why this armor was named after the Barabel [[Species/Legends|species]].
